item.title: Show HN: Enlarger • A local upscaler that keeps detail instead of smoothing it
item.summary: Hey everyone! I know there are gazillion alternatives out there especially with AI and all, but this nevertheless is my take on it. I created it because I was frustrated with the options I was seeing in the market. Too heavy, too complex, UX was meh, the output created was not resonating with my style of photography, they are generally glitchy or hallucinate too much, and I dislike subscription fees or credits personally. I prefer BIFL licenses and desktop apps. Since most will probably compare it to the free and open source Upscayl, I went ahead and did a comparison post on the visual output, which might explain more in depth on my motivations why I did Enlarger. You can find it from the "Blog" section. Would like to know what do you think of it, and if there are any questions about the tech stack or the whole process, I will try my best to answer!
